content.text:
Chemistry Lab Sessions 2 – Thermodynamics & Chemical Kinetics
Semester:
2  |
Teaching Unit:
Methodology Unit (MU)
Course Description
This laboratory course consolidates the theoretical knowledge acquired in the Thermodynamics & Chemical Kinetics module (Chemistry 2). Students will gain hands-on experience in performing experiments related to thermodynamic properties of substances, energy transformations, and reaction kinetics. The course emphasizes experimental techniques, data acquisition, analysis, and interpretation to strengthen understanding of physical chemistry concepts.
Learning Outcomes
Measure the heat capacity of liquids accurately.
Determine thermodynamic properties of ideal gases.
Measure the ratio of specific heats of a gas experimentally.
Apply the first law of thermodynamics in experimental contexts.
Conduct kinetic studies of reactions such as sucrose inversion, ester saponification, and hydrogen peroxide decomposition.
Analyze and interpret experimental results, comparing them with theoretical predictions.
Lab Schedule and Topics
Thermodynamics
Measurement of the heat capacity of liquids
Thermodynamic properties of ideal gases
Measurement of the ratio of specific heats of a gas
First law of thermodynamics
Kinetics
Sucrose inversion
Ester saponification (second-order reaction)
Decomposition of hydrogen peroxide
Assessment Method
Continuous assessment: 50%
Final exam: 50%
References
Mauduit, R. – Thermodynamics in 20 Summaries. Dunod (2013).
Fremaux, B. – Elements of Kinetics and Catalysis. Tec & Doc (1989).
Diu, B., et al. – Thermodynamics. Hermann, Paris (2007).
